message:
perf(channel): replace watch channel Mutex-guarded counters with atomics

WatchInner previously guarded two simple scalar values behind separate
Mutexes:
- receiver_count: Mutex<usize> — incremented on clone/subscribe,
  decremented on drop, read on every send/modify/is_closed check
- sender_dropped: Mutex<bool> — set once on Sender drop, read on
  every receiver poll

Both are classic read-heavy/write-rare patterns where Mutex contention
is pure overhead. This commit replaces them:

- receiver_count → AtomicUsize with Acquire/Release/AcqRel ordering
  - send()/modify(): load(Acquire) to check if anyone is listening
  - subscribe()/Receiver::clone(): fetch_add(1, AcqRel)
  - Receiver::drop(): fetch_sub(1, AcqRel)
  - receiver_count()/is_closed(): load(Acquire)

- sender_dropped → AtomicBool with Acquire/Release ordering
  - Sender::drop(): store(true, Release)
  - is_sender_dropped(): load(Acquire)

The previous Sender/Receiver Drop impls used defensive if-let locking
to handle the case where a Mutex was already poisoned during stack
unwinding (to avoid double-panic abort). With atomics this concern
disappears entirely — atomic stores never poison.

Tests updated:
- sender_drop_on_poisoned_mutex_does_not_panic → renamed to
  sender_drop_sets_sender_dropped_atomically (verifies atomic flag)
- receiver_drop_on_poisoned_mutex_does_not_panic → renamed to
  receiver_drop_decrements_count_atomically (verifies atomic counter)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
